name: Separation of Concerns
def: Dividing a system so each part addresses one distinct concern with minimal overlap.
problem: Limit the cognitive and change-impact scope of any one module by isolating responsibilities.
mechanism: Group related responsibilities, separate unrelated ones behind interfaces/layers/modules, so a change to one concern touches one place.
limits: Drawing the right boundaries is hard
failures: Boundaries that cut through a single concern
whenNot: Tiny scripts where structure adds noise
myths: "More files = better separation" — separation is about responsibility, not file count
